title: Juno Bellweather
content:
Juno Bellweather (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as franchise player of the Ironquay Forge when they won the interregional playoff crown in 1832.

Legacy.

Historians of the Ironquay Forge cite Juno Bellweather's 1832 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Kestrel Bay Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Championship season.

The 1832 interregional playoff crown campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Juno Bellweather averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Kestrel Bay Memorial Arena in Kestrel Bay.

Playing style.

As franchise player, Juno Bellweather preferred structured half-court sets and emphasized defensive rotations. Teammates credited his communication when the Ironquay Forge switched to zone coverage late in quarters.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the interregional playoff crown, Juno Bellweather remained with the Ironquay Forge for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Awards and honors.

Following the interregional playoff crown, Juno Bellweather received Finals Most Valuable Player recognition and was named to the All-League first team.
